objects.extract: In American English, a pitcher is a container with a handle and a spout, used for storing and pouring liquids. In English-speaking countries outside North America, such a container is called a jug.

objects.title: Reza Abbasi
objects.extract: Reza Abbasi (Persian: رضا عباسی), also known as Agha Reza (c. 1565 – 1635), was the leading Persian miniaturist of the Isfahan School during the later Safavid period, spending most of his career working for Shah Abbas I. He is considered to be the last great master of the Persian miniature, best known for his single miniatures for muraqqa or albums, especial

objects.title: Creamer
objects.description: cream pot at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 33.120.318)

objects.title: Soup
objects.extract: Soup is a primarily liquid food, generally served warm or hot – though it is sometimes served chilled – made by cooking or otherwise combining meat or vegetables with stock, milk, or water. According to The Oxford Companion to Food, soup is the main generic term for liquid savoury dishes; others include broth, bisque, consommé, potage and many more.

objects.title: Tomato frog
objects.extract: Tomato frogs are any of the three species of genus Dyscophus (family Microhylidae): D. antongilii, D. insularis, or D. guineti. Dyscophus is the only genus in subfamily Dyscophinae.
objects.artist: The original uploader was Firereptiles at English Wikipedia.

objects.title: Drip irrigation
objects.extract: Drip irrigation or trickle irrigation is a type of micro-irrigation system that has the potential to save water and nutrients by allowing water to drip slowly to the roots of plants, either from above the soil surface or buried below the surface.  The goal is to place water directly into the root zone and minimize evaporation.
objects.artist: No machine-readable author provided. Blumenfischer~commonswiki assumed (based on

objects.title: Böhme's Gecko
objects.extract: Böhme's gecko (Tarentola boehmei), also commonly known as the Morocco wall gecko, is a species of lizard in the family Phyllodactylidae. The species is native to western North Africa, where it is found in rocky areas such as cliffs and walls as well as ruins of buildings and urban areas.

objects.title: Trillium decumbens
objects.extract: Trillium decumbens, also known as the decumbent trillium or trailing wakerobin, is a species of flowering plant in the bunchflower family (Melanthiaceae). It is native to the southeastern United States, specifically Tennessee, Georgia, and Alabama, where it grows in mature deciduous woodlands or on open rocky wooded slopes.

objects.title: Folding screen
objects.extract: A folding screen, also known as pingfeng (Chinese: 屏風; pinyin: píngfēng), is a type of free-standing furniture consisting of several frames or panels, which are often connected by hinges or by other means. They have practical and decorative uses, and can be made in a variety of designs with different kinds of materials.

objects.title: Ensisheim
objects.description: Stony meteorite which landed outside Ensisheim in present-day Alsace, France (1492)
objects.extract: The Ensisheim meteorite is a stony meteorite that fell on November 7, 1492 in a wheat field outside the walled town of Ensisheim in then Alsace, Further Germany (now France). The meteorite can still be seen in Ensisheim's museum, the sixteenth-century Musée de la Régence.

objects.title: Lytechinus variegatus
objects.extract: Lytechinus variegatus, commonly called the green sea urchin  or the variegated sea urchin, is a species of sea urchin that can be found in the warm waters of the western Atlantic Ocean and Caribbean Sea.

objects.title: Sea anemone
objects.description: Marine animals of the order Actiniaria
objects.extract: Sea anemones (  ə-NEM-ə-nee) are a group of predatory marine invertebrate animals constituting the order Actiniaria. Because of their colourful appearance, they are named after the Anemone, a terrestrial flowering plant.

objects.title: Knife
objects.description: Tool or weapon with a cutting edge or blade
objects.extract: A knife (pl.: knives; from Old Norse knifr 'knife, dirk') is a tool or weapon with a cutting edge or blade, usually attached to a handle or hilt. One of the earliest tools used by humanity, knives appeared at least 2.5 million years ago, as evidenced by the Oldowan tools.
objects.artist:
Yegor Chernyshev at en.wikipedia.

Later version(s) were uploaded by Materialsci

objects.title: Sandal
objects.description: Type of footwear with an open upper
objects.extract: Sandals are an open type of footwear consisting of a sole held to the wearer's foot by straps that go over the instep and around the ankle. Sandals may also feature a heel of varying heights.

objects.title: Kevlar
objects.description: Heat-resistant and strong aromatic polyamide fiber
objects.extract: Kevlar (para-aramid) is a strong, heat-resistant synthetic fiber, related to other aramids such as Nomex and Technora. Developed by Stephanie Kwolek at DuPont in 1965, the high-strength material was first used commercially in the early 1970s as a replacement for steel in racing tires.
objects.artist: Author:Franklin Vera Pacheco / Franklin.vp at en.wikipedia

objects.title: European Union
objects.extract: The European Union (EU) is a political and economic union of 27 member states that are located primarily in Europe. A supranational union with a total area of 4,233,255 km2 (1,634,469 sq mi) and an estimated population of over 450 million as of 2025, its member states generated a nominal gross domestic product (GDP) of around €18.802 trillion in 2025, accoun

objects.title: Sisu (candy)
objects.extract:
Sisu is a Finnish brand of candy, currently produced by Leaf International.
Sisu was introduced to the market in 1928 by the Turku candy company Tehdas-Osakeyhtiö Seres (later Oy Seres Ab).

objects.title: Shrimp and prawn as food
objects.extract: Shrimps and prawns are types of crustacean seafood that are consumed worldwide. Prawns and shrimps are crustacea and are very similar in appearance with the terms often used interchangeably in commercial farming and wild fisheries.

objects.title: Cabbage
objects.description: Leafy vegetable in the flowering plant family Brassicaceae
objects.extract: Cabbage, comprising several cultivars of Brassica oleracea, is a leafy green, red (purple), or white (pale green) biennial plant grown as an annual vegetable crop for its dense-leaved heads. It is descended from the wild cabbage (B. oleracea var.

objects.title: Pterophanes cyanopterus
objects.extract: The great sapphirewing (Pterophanes cyanopterus) is a species of hummingbird in the "brilliants", tribe Heliantheini in subfamily Lesbiinae. It is found in Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru.
objects.artist: Michael Woodruff from Spokane, Washington, USA

objects.title: Automatic quartz
objects.extract: Automatic quartz is a collective term describing watch movements that combine a self-winding rotor mechanism (as used in automatic mechanical watches) to generate electricity with a piezoelectric quartz crystal as its timing element. Such movements aim to provide the advantages of quartz without the inconvenience and environmental impact of batteries.

objects.title: Trolleybus
objects.description: Electric bus taking power from overhead wires
objects.extract: A trolleybus (also known as trolley bus, trolley coach, trackless trolley, trackless tram (in the 1910s and 1920s), road tram or simply trolley) is an electric bus that draws power from dual overhead lines (generally suspended from roadside posts) using spring-loaded or pneumatically raised trolley poles. Two wires, and two trolley poles, are required to com

objects.title: Peaked cap
objects.extract: A peaked cap, peaked hat, service cap, barracks cover, forage cap, or combination cap is a form of headgear worn by the armed forces of many nations, as well as many uniformed civilian organisations such as law enforcement agencies and fire departments. It derives its name from its short visor, or peak, which was historically made of polished leather but inc

objects.title: Drum
objects.description: instrument in the collections of the Swedish Museum of Performing Arts (N90388)

objects.title: Pump organ
objects.extract: The pump organ or reed organ is a type of organ that uses free reeds to generate sound, with air passing over vibrating thin metal strips mounted in a frame. Types include the pressure-based harmonium, the suction reed organ (which employs a vacuum system), and the Indian harmonium.

objects.title: Gorilla
objects.extract: Gorillas are large, primarily herbivorous, great apes that live in the tropical forests of equatorial Africa. The genus Gorilla is divided into two species: the eastern gorilla and the western gorilla, and either four or five subspecies.

objects.title: Outline of vehicles
objects.description: Overview of and topical guide to vehicles
objects.extract:
The following outline is provided as an overview of and topical guide to vehicles:

Vehicle – non-living means of transportation.  Vehicles are most often human-made, although some other means of transportation which are not made by humans can also be called vehicles; examples include icebergs and floating tree trunks.

objects.title: Mochi
objects.extract: Mochi ( MOH-chee; Japanese もち, 餅 [motɕi] ) is a Japanese rice cake made of mochigome (もち米), a short-grain japonica glutinous rice, and sometimes other ingredients such as water, sugar, and cornstarch. The steamed rice is pounded into paste and molded into the desired shape.

objects.title: Death growl
objects.extract: The death growl, growl, or sometimes Cookie Monster growl, is an extended vocal technique usually employed in extreme styles of music, particularly in death metal and other extreme subgenres of heavy metal music. Sometimes death growl vocals are criticized for their "ugliness" and inability to be understood without an accompanying lyric sheet, but the presen

objects.title: Oatmeal
objects.extract: Oatmeal is a preparation of oats that have been dehusked, steamed, and flattened, or a coarse flour of hulled oat grains (groats) that have either been milled (ground), rolled, or steel-cut. Ground oats are also called white oats.

objects.title: Soup
objects.extract: Soup is a primarily liquid food, generally served warm or hot – though it is sometimes served chilled – made by cooking or otherwise combining meat or vegetables with stock, milk, or water. According to The Oxford Companion to Food, soup is the main generic term for liquid savoury dishes; others include broth, bisque, consommé, potage and many more.
objects.artist: Pasquale Paolo Cardo from Finale Ligure (Savona), Italy

objects.title: Steel wool
objects.description: Bundle of very fine and flexible sharp-edged steel filaments
objects.extract: Steel wool, also known as iron wool or wire sponge, is a bundle of very fine and flexible sharp-edged steel filaments. It is the most common type of wire wool and is often the type meant when wire wool is mentioned.

objects.title: Sugar substitute
objects.description: Sugarless food additive intended to provide a sweet taste
objects.extract: A sugar substitute or artificial sweetener is a food additive that provides a sweetness like that of sugar while containing significantly less food energy than sugar-based sweeteners, making it a zero-calorie (non-nutritive) or low-calorie sweetener. Artificial sweeteners may be derived from plant extracts or processed by chemical synthesis.

objects.title: Potato
objects.description: Starchy tuber used as a staple food
objects.extract: The potato () is a starchy tuberous vegetable native to the Americas that is consumed as a staple food in many parts of the world. Potatoes are underground stem tubers of the plant Solanum tuberosum, a perennial in the nightshade family Solanaceae.

objects.title: Tyrkisk peber
objects.extract:
Tyrkisk peber (Danish for "Turkish pepper", often referred to as Turkinpippuri in Finnish, Türkisch Pfeffer in German, Tyrkisk pepper in Norwegian and Turkisk peppar in Swedish) is a brand of salty liquorice candy flavoured with salmiac, popular in Northern Europe.
The brand was first launched in 1977 by the Danish company Perelly, but was acquired the Finni

objects.title: Vending machine
objects.description: Machine which dispenses products to customers
objects.extract: A vending machine is an automated machine that dispenses items such as snacks, beverages, cigarettes, and lottery tickets to consumers after cash, a credit card, or other forms of payment are inserted into the machine or payment is otherwise made. The first modern vending machines were developed in England in the early 1880s and dispensed postcards.
objects.artist: Sean Kimmons, U.S. Army Garrison Japan Public Affairs

objects.title: Freediving
objects.extract:
Freediving, free-diving, free diving, breath-hold diving, or skin diving is a mode of underwater diving that relies on breath-holding (apnea) until resurfacing rather than the use of breathing apparatus such as scuba gear.
Besides the limit of breath-hold, immersion in water and exposure to high ambient pressure also have physiological effects that limit the

objects.title: Mitre box
objects.description: Woodworking tool used to guide a saw
objects.extract: A mitre box or miter box (American English) is a wood working appliance used to guide a hand saw for making precise cuts, usually 45° mitre cuts. Traditional mitre boxes are simple in construction and made of wood, while adjustable mitre boxes are made of metal and can be adjusted for cutting any angle from 45° to 90°.

objects.title: Rail transport
objects.extract: Rail transport (also known as train transport) is a means of transport using wheeled vehicles running on tracks, which usually consist of two parallel steel rails. Rail transport is one of the two primary means of land transport, next to road transport.

objects.title: Billiard ball
objects.extract: A billiard ball is a small, hard ball used in cue sports, such as carom billiards, pool, and snooker. The number, type, diameter, color, and pattern of the balls differ depending upon the specific game being played.

objects.title: Chandelier
objects.extract: A chandelier () is an ornamental lighting device, typically with spreading branched supports for multiple lights, designed to be hung from the ceiling. Chandeliers are often ornate, and they were originally designed to hold candles, but now incandescent light bulbs as well as fluorescent lamps and LEDs are commonly used.

objects.title: Thornycroft J Type
objects.extract: The Thornycroft J Type was a British truck built by the Thornycroft company, it saw widespread service with the British military and Imperial forces during the First World War.

objects.title: Achille Castiglioni
objects.extract: Achille Castiglioni (Italian pronunciation: [aˈkille kastiʎˈʎoːni]; 1918 – 2002) was an Italian architect and designer. He is known for his furniture, lighting, radiograms and other objects, many of which are considered icons of post-war Italian design.

objects.title: Bible
objects.extract: A Bible is a collection of religious texts that is central to Christianity or Judaism, and esteemed in other Abrahamic religions such as Islam. Bibles are anthologies (compilations of texts of a variety of forms) with varying canonical texts across traditions.

objects.title: Centaurea cyanus
objects.extract: Centaurea cyanus, commonly known as cornflower or bachelor's button (among other names), is an annual flowering plant in the family Asteraceae native to Europe. In the past, it often grew as a weed in cornfields (in the broad sense of "corn", referring to grains, such as wheat, barley, rye, or oats), hence its name.

objects.title: Artocarpus heterophyllus
objects.extract:
The jackfruit, jakfruit or nangka (Artocarpus heterophyllus) is a species of tree in the fig, mulberry, and breadfruit family (Moraceae).
The jackfruit is the largest tree fruit, reaching as much as 55 kg (120 pounds) in weight, 90 cm (35 inches) in length, and 50 cm (20 inches) in diameter.

objects.title: Beer bottle
objects.description: Bottle designed as a container for beer
objects.extract: A beer bottle is a bottle designed as a container for beer. Such designs vary greatly in size and shape, but the glass commonly is brown or green to reduce spoilage from light, especially ultraviolet.

objects.title: Door handle
objects.description: Device to open or close door
objects.extract: A door handle or doorknob is a handle used to open or close a door. Door handles can be found on all types of doors including: exterior doors of residential and commercial buildings, internal doors, cupboard doors and vehicle doors.

objects.title: Camel racing
objects.description: Popular sport in parts of Africa, Asia and Australia
objects.extract: Camel racing is a form of racing in which jockeys riding on camels compete against each other to finish a set number of laps around a circular racetrack. It is most popular in the Middle East, the Horn of Africa, Pakistan, Mongolia and Australia.

objects.title: Batik
objects.extract: Batik is a dyeing technique using wax resist. The term is also used to describe patterned textiles created with that technique.

objects.title: Papaya
objects.extract:
The papaya (, US: ) is the plant species Carica papaya, one of the 21 accepted species in the genus Carica of the family Caricaceae.
It was first domesticated in Mesoamerica, within modern-day southern Mexico and Central America.

objects.title: Barrel
objects.extract: A barrel or cask is a hollow cylindrical container with a bulging center, longer than it is wide. They are traditionally made of wooden staves and bound by wooden or metal hoops.

objects.title: Baritone horn
objects.extract: The baritone horn, or often simply the baritone, is a valved brass instrument pitched in B♭ in the saxhorn family, employed chiefly in brass, military and concert bands. In North America for most of the 20th century, the term baritone also referred to similar instruments with a wider-bore closer to the euphonium.
objects.artist: Auckland Museum  Collections from Auckland, Aotearoa New Zealand

objects.title: Dragon's beard candy
objects.extract: Dragon's beard candy (traditional Chinese: 龍鬚糖; simplified Chinese: 龙须糖; pinyin: lóng xū táng) or Chinese cotton candy or Longxusu (simplified Chinese: 龙须酥; traditional Chinese: 龍鬚酥; pinyin: lóng xū sū) is a handmade traditional Chinese confectionary similar to floss halva or Western cotton candy. Dragon's beard candy was initially created in China, but was
objects.artist: No machine-readable author provided. Dgh assumed (based on copyright claims).

objects.title: Black-tailed Godwit
objects.extract: The black-tailed godwit (Limosa limosa) is a large, long-legged, long-billed shorebird first described by Carl Linnaeus in 1758. It is a member of the godwit genus, Limosa.

objects.title: Otter
objects.extract: Otters are carnivorous mammals in the subfamily Lutrinae. The 14 extant otter species are all semiaquatic, both freshwater and marine.

objects.title: Bottle
objects.extract: A bottle is a narrow-necked container made of an impermeable material (such as glass, pottery, porcelain, plastic or aluminium) in various shapes and sizes that stores and transports liquids. Its mouth, at the bottling line, can be sealed with an internal stopper, an external bottle cap, a closure, or induction sealing.

objects.title: Intarsia
objects.extract: Intarsia is a form of wood inlaying that is similar to marquetry. The practice dates from before the 7th century AD. The technique inserts sections of wood (at times with contrasting ivory or bone, or mother-of-pearl) within the solid wood matrix of floors and walls or of tabletops and other furniture; by contrast marquetry assembles a pattern out of veneers

objects.title: bolus
objects.extract: The bolus or baulus is a brioche pastry based on flour, milk, butter, cassonade and raisins, in spiral shape. It is a Belgian and especially Brussels speciality, similar to Zeeuwse bolus but with raisins.

objects.title: Stand
objects.description: stand at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 1997.34.23)

objects.title: Car
objects.extract: A car, or an automobile, is a motor vehicle with wheels. Most definitions of cars state that they run primarily on roads, seat 1-8 people, have four wheels, and mainly transport people rather than cargo.

objects.title: List of chicken breeds
objects.extract: There are hundreds of chicken breeds in existence. Domesticated for thousands of years, distinguishable breeds of chicken have been present since the combined factors of geographical isolation and selection for desired characteristics created regional types with distinct physical and behavioral traits passed on to their offspring.

objects.title: Thangka
objects.extract: A thangka painting  (Nepali pronunciation: [ˈt̪ʰaŋka]; Tibetan: ཐང་ཀ་; Nepal Bhasa: पौभा) is a Tibetan Buddhist painting on cotton, silk appliqué, usually depicting a Buddhist deity, scene, or mandala. Thangkas are traditionally kept unframed and rolled up when not on display, mounted on a textile backing somewhat in the style of Chinese scroll paintings, wi

objects.title: Lusorex taishanensis
objects.extract: Lusorex is an extinct genus of heterosoricid shrew that lived in China during the Miocene. The only species is L. taishanensis, fossils of which are kept at the Paleozoological Museum of China.

objects.title: Ramen
objects.extract: Ramen () (拉麺, ラーメン or らあめん, rāmen; [ɾaꜜːmeɴ] ) is a Japanese noodle dish. It is a part of Japanese Chinese cuisine.

objects.title: Crassignatha changyan
objects.artist: Li Y, Lin Y, Li S (2020) A review of Crassignatha (Araneae, Symphytognathidae).

objects.title: propellant depot
objects.description: Cache of propellant used to refuel spacecraft
objects.extract: An orbital propellant depot is a cache of propellant that is placed in orbit around Earth or another body to allow spacecraft or the transfer stage of the spacecraft to be fueled in space. It is one of the types of space resource depots that have been proposed for enabling infrastructure-based space exploration.

objects.title: Reindeer
objects.extract: The reindeer or caribou (Rangifer tarandus) is a species of deer with circumpolar distribution, native to Arctic, subarctic, tundra, boreal, and mountainous regions of Northern Europe, Siberia, and North America. It is the only representative of the genus Rangifer.

objects.title: Ameletus cryptostimulus
objects.extract: Ameletus cryptostimulus is a species of combmouthed minnow mayfly in the family Ameletidae. It is found in southeastern Canada and the eastern United States.
objects.artist: Bob Henricks from Charlottesville, United States

objects.title: Supermodel
objects.extract: A supermodel is a highly paid fashion model who has a worldwide reputation and background in haute couture and commercial modeling. The term became popular in the 1990s.

objects.title: Pascagoula map turtle
objects.extract: The Pascagoula map turtle (Graptemys gibbonsi) is a species of turtle in the family Emydidae. The species is native to the southern United States.

objects.title: Houseplant
objects.description: Ornamental plant in a home or office
objects.extract: A houseplant, also known as a pot plant, potted plant, or indoor plant, is an ornamental plant cultivated indoors for aesthetic or practical purposes. These plants are commonly found in homes, offices, and various indoor spaces, where they contribute to the ambiance by adding natural beauty and improving air quality.

objects.title: Roman basin from Montfort
objects.artist: Ricardo Tulio Gandelman from Rio de Janeiro, Brazil

objects.title: Steam engine
objects.description: Engine that uses steam to perform mechanical work
objects.extract: A steam engine is a heat engine that performs mechanical work using steam as its working fluid. The steam engine uses the force produced by steam pressure to push a piston back and forth inside a cylinder.
objects.artist: 04_15_09_162xp_-_Flickr_-_drewj1946.jpg: Drew  Jacksich from San Jose, CA, The R

objects.title: Lacquerware
objects.extract: Lacquerware are objects decoratively covered with lacquer. Lacquerware includes small or large containers, tableware, miscellaneous lacquered small objects carried by people, and larger objects such as furniture and even coffins painted with lacquer.

objects.title: Late antiquity
objects.description: Post-classical antiquity in western Eurasia and Northern Africa
objects.extract: Late antiquity is a time period between classical antiquity and the Early Middle Ages. The exact start and end dates are debatable.

objects.title: Conch
objects.extract: Conch (US:  KONK, KONCH, UK:  KONCH) is any of a number of different medium-to-large-sized sea snails. Conch shells typically have a high spire and a noticeable siphonal canal (in other words, the shell comes to a noticeable point on both ends).

objects.title: Azulejo
objects.extract: Azulejo (Portuguese: [ɐzuˈle(j)ʒu, ɐzuˈlɐjʒu], Spanish: [aθuˈlexo]; from the Arabic الزليج, al-zillīj) is a form of Iberian painted tin-glazed ceramic tilework. They are an ornamental art form, but also had a specific function, such as aiding temperature control in homes.

objects.title: Tefillin
objects.description: Leather boxes containing parchment with Torah verses
objects.extract: Tefillin (Hebrew: תְּפִלִּין or תְּפִילִּין; Ashkenazi and Modern Israeli Hebrew pronunciation: [tfiˈlin]), or phylacteries, are sets of small black leather boxes with leather straps containing scrolls of parchment inscribed with verses from the Torah. Tefillin are traditionally worn by male adult Jews during Shacharit on weekdays.

objects.title: White House
objects.description: Residence and workplace of the US president
objects.extract: The White House is the official residence and workplace of the president of the United States. Located at 1600 Pennsylvania Avenue NW in Washington, D.C., it has served as the residence of every U.S. president since John Adams in 1800 when the national capital was moved from Philadelphia.

objects.title: Bartender
objects.description: Person behind the bar who serves (usually alcoholic) beverages
objects.extract: A bartender (also known as a barkeep, barman, barmaid or mixologist) is a person who formulates and serves alcoholic or soft drink beverages behind the bar, usually in a licensed establishment as well as in restaurants and nightclubs, but also occasionally at private parties. Bartenders also usually maintain the supplies and inventory for the bar.

objects.title: Reza Abbasi
objects.extract: Reza Abbasi (Persian: رضا عباسی), also known as Agha Reza (c. 1565 – 1635), was the leading Persian miniaturist of the Isfahan School during the later Safavid period, spending most of his career working for Shah Abbas I. He is considered to be the last great master of the Persian miniature, best known for his single miniatures for muraqqa or albums, especial

objects.title: Bop It
objects.extract: Bop It, stylized as bop it! since 2008, is a line of audio game toys.

objects.title: Polar bear
objects.description: Species of bear native to the Arctic
objects.extract: The polar bear (Ursus maritimus) is a large bear native to the Arctic and nearby areas. It is closely related to the brown bear, and the two species can interbreed.

objects.title: Pointed hat
objects.extract: Pointed hats have been a form of headgear of a wide range of cultures throughout history. Although often suggesting an ancient Indo-European tradition, they were also traditionally worn by women of Lapland, the Japanese, the Mi'kmaq people of Atlantic Canada, and the Huastecs of Veracruz and Aztec (e.g., as illustrated in the Codex Mendoza).
objects.artist: No machine-readable author provided. Carlomorino assumed (based on copyright cla

objects.title: Salamander
objects.extract: Salamanders are a group of amphibians typically characterized by their lizard-like appearance, with slender bodies, blunt snouts, short limbs projecting at right angles to the body, and the presence of a tail in both larvae and adults. All ten extant salamander families are grouped together under the order Urodela, the sole surviving order from the group Cau

objects.title: Catholic Church
objects.extract: The Catholic Church (Latin: Ecclesia Catholica), also called the Roman Catholic Church, is the largest Christian church, with an estimated 1.28 to 1.41 billion baptized members worldwide as of 2026. It consists of 24 autonomous (sui iuris) churches—the Latin Church and 23 Eastern Catholic Churches—organized into nearly 3,500 dioceses and eparchies governed b

objects.title: List of pasta
objects.extract: There are many different varieties of pasta. They are usually sorted by size, being long (pasta lunga), short (pasta corta), stuffed (ripiena), cooked in broth (pastina), stretched (strascinati) or in dumpling-like form (gnocchi/gnocchetti).

objects.title: Shripech
objects.description: Royal Crown of the Kingdom of Nepal
objects.extract: The Shripech or Shreepech (Nepali: श्रीपेच) was the coronation crown of the Kingdom of Nepal, traditionally worn by Nepalese monarchs. During the Rana rule, a special variant of the Shripech, known as the Rana crown, was also created for members of the aristocratic Rana family to wear as a ceremonial headpiece.

objects.title: Loader (equipment)
objects.extract: A loader is a heavy equipment machine used in construction to move or load materials such as soil, rock, sand, demolition debris, etc. into or onto another type of machinery (such as a dump truck, conveyor belt, feed-hopper, or railroad car).

objects.title: Adidas
objects.description: German multinational clothing and apparel corporation
objects.extract: Adidas AG (German pronunciation: [ˈadiˌdas] ; styled as adidas since 1949) is a German multinational athletic apparel and footwear corporation headquartered in Herzogenaurach, Germany. It is the largest sportswear manufacturer in Europe, and the second largest in the world, after Nike.

objects.title: Tall Clock
objects.description: tall clock at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 1986.82)

objects.title: Thangka
objects.extract: A thangka painting  (Nepali pronunciation: [ˈt̪ʰaŋka]; Tibetan: ཐང་ཀ་; Nepal Bhasa: पौभा) is a Tibetan Buddhist painting on cotton, silk appliqué, usually depicting a Buddhist deity, scene, or mandala. Thangkas are traditionally kept unframed and rolled up when not on display, mounted on a textile backing somewhat in the style of Chinese scroll paintings, wi

objects.title: Lorgnette
objects.description: Pair of spectacles with a handle
objects.extract: A lorgnette () is a pair of spectacles with a handle, used to hold them in place, rather than fitting over the ears or nose. The word lorgnette is derived from the French lorgner, to take a sidelong look at, and Middle French, from lorgne, squinting.

objects.title: Jason Heyward
objects.extract: Jason Alias Heyward (born August 9, 1989), nicknamed "J-Hey", is an American former professional baseball right fielder. He played 16 seasons in Major League Baseball (MLB) for the Atlanta Braves, St.
objects.artist: Julie Fennell on Flickr (Original version)  UCinternational (Crop)

objects.title: Coral reef
objects.description: Outcrop of rock in the sea formed by the growth and deposit of stony coral skeletons
objects.extract: A coral reef is an underwater ecosystem characterized by reef-building corals. Reefs are formed of colonies of coral polyps held together by calcium carbonate.

objects.title: Kitten
objects.extract: A kitten is a juvenile cat. After being born, kittens display primary altriciality and are fully dependent on their mothers for survival.

objects.title: Hooded Vulture
objects.extract: The  hooded vulture (Necrosyrtes monachus) is an Old World vulture in the order Accipitriformes, which also includes eagles, kites, buzzards and hawks. It is the only member of the genus Necrosyrtes, which is sister to the larger Gyps genus, both of which are a part of the Aegypiinae subfamily of Old World vultures.

objects.title: Abronia cuetzpali
objects.extract: Abronia cuetzpali is a species of arboreal alligator lizard described in 2016 by Campbell, Solano-Zavaleta, Flores-Villela, Caviedes-Solís and Frost from the Sierra de Miahuatlán of Oaxaca, Mexico.

objects.title: Orange (fruit)
objects.extract: The orange, also called sweet orange to distinguish it from the bitter orange (Citrus × aurantium), is the fruit of a tree in the family Rutaceae. Botanically, this is the hybrid Citrus × sinensis, between the pomelo (Citrus maxima) and the mandarin orange (Citrus reticulata).

objects.title: Bergère
objects.extract: A bergère (French for shepherdess) is an enclosed upholstered French armchair (fauteuil) with an upholstered back and armrests on upholstered frames. The seat frame is over-upholstered, but the rest of the wooden framing is exposed: it may be moulded or carved, and of beech, painted or gilded, or of fruitwood, walnut or mahogany with a waxed finish.

objects.title: Pepin the Short
objects.description: King of the Franks from 751 to 768
objects.extract: Pepin the Short (Latin: Pipinus; French: Pépin le Bref; German: Pippin der Kurze; c. 714 – 24 September 768) was King of the Franks from 751 until his death in 768. He was the first Carolingian to become king.

objects.title: Boycott
objects.description: Voluntary abstention from using a product or service
objects.extract: A boycott is an act of nonviolent, voluntary abstention from a product, person, organization, or country as an expression of protest. It is usually for moral, social, political, or environmental reasons.

objects.title: Card table
objects.description: furniture highlighted in The MET collection

objects.title: Trout
objects.extract: Trout (pl.: trout) is a generic common name for numerous species of carnivorous freshwater fishes belonging to the genera Oncorhynchus, Salmo, and Salvelinus, all of which are members of the subfamily Salmoninae in the family Salmonidae. The word trout is also used for some similar-shaped but non-salmonid fish, such as the spotted seatrout/speckled trout (Cy

objects.title: Kurt Suzuki
objects.extract: Kurtis Kiyoshi Suzuki (Japanese: 鈴木 清, romanized: Suzuki Kiyoshi, born October 4, 1983) is an American baseball manager and former catcher who is the manager for the Los Angeles Angels of Major League Baseball (MLB). He played in MLB for the Oakland Athletics, Washington Nationals, Minnesota Twins, Atlanta Braves, and Angels.
objects.artist: BADBOY_1975 on フォト蔵 (Original version)  UCinternational (Crop)

objects.title: Goose
objects.description: Common name for a group of waterfowl
objects.extract: A goose (pl.: geese) is a bird of any of several waterfowl species in the family Anatidae. This group comprises the genera Anser (grey geese and white geese) and Branta (black geese).

objects.title: FOS.328
objects.description: Asteracanthus ornatissimus fossil from Fish collection of Louis Agassiz in Natural history museum in Neuchâtel, Switzerland

objects.title: Dionysus
objects.description: Ancient Greek god of winemaking and wine
objects.extract: In ancient Greek religion and myth, Dionysus ( ; Ancient Greek: Διόνυσος Diónysos) is the god of wine-making, orchards, fruit, vegetation, fertility, festivity, insanity, ritual madness, religious ecstasy, and theatre. He was also known as Bacchus ( or ; Ancient Greek: Βάκχος Bacchos) by the Greeks (a name later adopted by the Romans) for a frenzy he is said

objects.title: Nazar (amulet)
objects.description: Eye-shaped amulet believed to protect against the evil eye
objects.extract: A naẓar (from Arabic ‏نَظَر‎ [ˈnaðˤar], meaning 'sight', 'surveillance', 'attention', and other related concepts), or an eye bead, is an eye-shaped amulet believed to protect against the evil eye. The term is also used in Albanian language  Azerbaijani, Bengali, Hebrew, Hindi–Urdu, Kurdish, Pashto, Persian, Punjabi, Turkish,  and other languages.

objects.title: Champús
objects.extract: Champús is a popular drink in South America, particularly Ecuador, Peru and southwest Colombia. It is made with corn, fruits (such as lulo, also known as naranjilla), pineapple, quince or guanábana, sweetened with panela and seasoned with cinnamon, cloves and orange tree leaves.

objects.title: Headband
objects.extract: A headband (also called a hairband or sweatband) is a clothing accessory worn in the hair or around the forehead, usually to hold hair away from the face or eyes, or to help control forehead sweat or (rarely) to provide some protection to the head during physical activity.  Headbands generally consist of a textile loop of elasticised and sometimes absorptive

objects.title: Crown of thorns
objects.extract: According to the New Testament, a woven crown of thorns (Ancient Greek: στέφανος ἐξ ἀκανθῶν, romanized: stephanos ex akanthōn or ἀκάνθινος στέφανος, akanthinos stephanos) was placed on the head of Jesus during the events leading up to his crucifixion. It was one of the instruments of the Passion, employed by Jesus' captors both to cause him pain and to mock
objects.artist:
This Photo was taken by Wolfgang Moroder.  

Feel free to use my photos, but ple

objects.title: Autogyro
objects.extract: An autogyro (from Greek αὐτός and γύρος, "self-turning"), gyroplane or gyrocopter, is a class of rotorcraft that uses an unpowered rotor in free autorotation to develop lift. A gyroplane "means a rotorcraft whose rotors are not engine-driven, except for initial starting, but are made to rotate by action of the air when the rotorcraft is moving; and whose mea

objects.title: Goat
objects.extract: The goat or domestic goat (Capra hircus) is a species of goat-antelope that is mostly kept as livestock. It was domesticated from the wild goat (C. aegagrus) of Southwest Asia and Eastern Europe.

objects.title: Embroidery
objects.description: Art or handicraft of decorating fabric or other materials with needle and thread or yarn
objects.extract: Embroidery is the art of decorating fabric or other materials using a needle to stitch thread or yarn. It is one of the oldest forms of textile art, with origins dating back thousands of years across various cultures.

objects.title: List of edible plants and mushrooms of southeast Alaska
Bullfighting
objects.description: Physical contest involving a matador and a bull
objects.extract: Bullfighting is a physical contest that involves a bullfighter attempting to subdue, immobilize, or kill a bull, usually according to a set of rules, guidelines, or cultural expectations. Bullfighting can also loosely refer to other spectacles in which two bulls are pitted against each other, that is, bull wrestling.
objects.artist: No machine-readable author provided. Josejasc-f~commonswiki assumed (based on co

objects.title: Summer Bird
objects.extract: Summer Bird (April 7, 2006 – December 23, 2013) was a champion American Thoroughbred racehorse, son of 2004 Belmont Stakes winner Birdstone. He was bred by retired cardiologist Kalarikkal Jayaraman and his wife, retired pathologist Vilasini Jayaraman, at their Tiffany Farm near Ocala, Florida.

objects.title: American sole
objects.extract: The American soles are a family (Achiridae) of flatfish occurring in both freshwater and marine environments of the Americas.  The family includes about 35 species in seven genera.

objects.title: Woodworking
objects.description: Process of making objects from wood
objects.extract: Woodworking is the skill of making items from wood, and includes cabinetry, furniture making, wood carving, joinery, carpentry, and woodturning.
objects.artist: Jacques from Cape Town, South Africa

objects.title: Shrimp and prawn as food
objects.extract: Shrimps and prawns are types of crustacean seafood that are consumed worldwide. Prawns and shrimps are crustacea and are very similar in appearance with the terms often used interchangeably in commercial farming and wild fisheries.

objects.title: Gee Bee Model Z Super Sportster
objects.extract: The Granville Gee Bee Model Z was an American racing aircraft that was built, successfully raced and then destroyed in a fatal crash in 1931. It was the first of the Super Sportster aircraft built by Granville Brothers Aircraft of Springfield, Massachusetts, with the sole intent of winning the Thompson Trophy, which it did in September 1931.

objects.title: Metropolitan Museum of Art
objects.description: Art museum in New York City
objects.extract: The Metropolitan Museum of Art, colloquially referred to as the Met, is an encyclopedic art museum in New York City. By floor area, it is the fourth-largest museum in the world and the largest art museum in the Americas.
objects.artist: Master of the Nativity of Castello

objects.title: Puppy
objects.extract:
A puppy is a juvenile dog, generally one less than 12–18 months old. 
Puppies are markedly underdeveloped and dependent on their mothers at birth (displaying altriciality), but healthy puppies grow quickly and begin walking thereafter.

objects.title: Motorcade
objects.description: Procession of official vehicles, often VIP limousines
objects.extract: A motorcade, or autocade, is a procession of motor vehicles, which can be used for ceremonial processions, funerals or demonstrations, or to provide security while transporting a very important person. The American presidential motorcade is an example of both and is a staple of public appearances by the president of the United States.

objects.title: Headscarf
objects.description: Piece of cloth worn on one's head
objects.extract: A headscarf is a scarf covering most or all of the top of a person's, usually women's, hair and head, leaving the face uncovered. A headscarf is formed of a triangular cloth or a square cloth folded into a triangle, with which the head is covered.

objects.title: Damselfly
objects.extract: Damselflies are flying insects of the suborder Zygoptera in the order Odonata. They are similar to dragonflies (which constitute the other odonatan suborder, Epiprocta) but are usually smaller and have slimmer bodies.

objects.title: Measuring cup
objects.description: Kitchen utensil to measure volume of cooking ingredients
objects.extract: A measuring cup is a kitchen utensil used primarily to measure the volume of liquid or bulk solid cooking ingredients such as flour and sugar, especially for volumes from about 50 mL (approx. 2 fl oz) upwards.

objects.title: Corrective lens
objects.description: Type of lens to improve visual perception
objects.extract: A corrective lens is a transmissive optical device that is worn on the eye to improve visual perception. The most common use is to treat refractive errors: myopia, hypermetropia, astigmatism, and presbyopia.

objects.title: Halva
objects.description: Confections often made from nut butters or flours
objects.extract: Halva (also halvah, halwa, halua) is a type of confectionery that is spread throughout  North Africa, East Africa, Eastern Europe, and the Balkans, West Asia, Central Asia, and South Asia. The name refers to a broad variety of recipes, generally a thick paste made from flour, butter, oil, saffron, rosewater, milk, turmeric powder, and sugar.

objects.title: Water distribution system
objects.description: Infrastructure to carry potable water to consumers
objects.extract: A water distribution system is a part of water supply network with components that carry potable water from a centralized treatment plant or wells to consumers to satisfy residential, commercial, industrial and fire fighting requirements.

objects.title: Gladiator
objects.extract: A gladiator (Latin: gladiator 'swordsman', from Latin  gladius 'sword') was an armed combatant who entertained audiences in the Roman Republic and Roman Empire in violent confrontations with other gladiators, wild animals, and condemned criminals. Some gladiators were volunteers who risked their lives and their legal and social standing by appearing in the a

objects.title: Tiffany lamp
objects.description: Type of lamp with colorful glass shade
objects.extract: A Tiffany lamp is a type of lamp with a glass shade designed by Louis Comfort Tiffany and collaborators, including Clara Driscoll, made in his design studio in Corona, Queens, New York City. The Tiffany glass in the lampshades is put together with the copper-foil technique instead of lead came, the classic technique for stained glass windows.

objects.title: Savannah cat
objects.extract: The Savannah is a breed of hybrid cat developed in the late 20th century from hybridisation of a serval (Leptailurus serval) with a domestic cat (Felis catus). This hybridisation typically produces large and lean offspring, with the serval's characteristic large ears and markedly black spotted coats.

objects.title: Candlestick
objects.description: Device used to hold a candle in place
objects.extract: A candlestick (or candleholder) is a device used to hold a candle upright in place. Most candlesticks have a cup, a spike (called a "pricket"), or both to secure the candle.

objects.title: denisovan
objects.extract: The Denisovans or Denisova hominins ( də-NIS-ə-və(nz)) are an extinct species or subspecies of archaic human that ranged across Asia during the Middle to Late Pleistocene, approximately 200,000–32,000 years ago. Most of what is known about Denisovans comes from DNA evidence.

objects.title: Cyclopina busanensis
objects.extract: Cyclopina busanensis is a species of copepod belonging to the order Cyclopoida, and the family, Cyclopinidae. The species was first described in 2020 by zoologist, Tomislav Karanovic.
objects.artist: Karanovic T (2020) Four new Cyclopina (Copepoda, Cyclopinidae) from South Korea.

objects.title: Camogie
objects.extract: Camogie (  kə-MOH-ghee; Irish: camógaíocht [kəˈmˠoːɡiːxt̪ˠ]) is an Irish stick-and-ball team sport played by women. It is played by 100,000 women in Ireland and worldwide, largely among Irish communities.
objects.artist: National Library of Ireland on The Commons

objects.title: 2016 Summer Olympics, Brazil v Australia 12 August 2016
Siamese fighting fish
objects.description: Species of fish native to Thailand
objects.extract: The Siamese fighting fish (Betta splendens), commonly known as the Siamese fighter, or locally as the betta, is a freshwater fish native to Southeast Asia, namely Cambodia, Laos, Myanmar, Malaysia, Thailand, and Vietnam. It is one of 76 species of the genus Betta, but the only one eponymously called "betta", owing to its global popularity as a pet; Betta spl

objects.title: Motor oil
objects.description: Lubricant used for internal combustion engines
objects.extract: Motor oil, engine oil, or engine lubricant is any one of various substances used for the lubrication of internal combustion engines. Motor oils typically consist of base oils enhanced with various additives, particularly antiwear additives, detergents, dispersants, and, for multi-grade oils, viscosity index improvers.

objects.title: Wooden fish
objects.extract: A wooden fish, also known as a muyu, moktak, mogeo, mokugyo, Chinese temple block, or wooden bell is a type of woodblock that originated from China that is used as a percussion instrument by monks and lay people in the Mahayana tradition of Buddhism.  They are used in Buddhist ceremonies in China, Korea, Japan, Vietnam and other Asian countries.

objects.title: Hachimaki
objects.extract: A hachimaki (鉢巻) (headband, lit. "helmet-scarf") is a type of Japanese headband, usually made of red or white cloth, typically featuring a design of kanji at the front.

objects.title: Fly system
objects.extract: A fly system, or theatrical rigging system, is a system of ropes, pulleys, counterweights and related devices within a theater that enables a stage crew to quickly, quietly and safely fly (hoist) components such as curtains, lights, scenery, stage effects and, sometimes, people. Systems are typically designed to fly components between clear view of the audie

objects.title: Pteronotus personatus
objects.extract: Wagner's mustached bat (Pteronotus personatus) is a bat species from South and Central America. It is one of the few New World bats species known to perform Doppler shift compensation behavior.

objects.title: Merlin (console)
objects.extract:
Merlin (also known as Merlin The Electronic Wizard, stylized as MERLIN) is a handheld electronic game first made by Parker Brothers in 1978.
The game was invented by former NASA employee Bob Doyle, his wife Holly, and brother-in-law Wendl Thomis.

objects.title: Street name sign
objects.description: Type of traffic sign used to identify named roads
objects.extract: A street name sign is a type of traffic sign used to identify named roads, generally those that do not qualify as expressways or highways. Street name signs are most often found posted at intersections; sometimes, especially in the United States, in perpendicularly oriented pairs identifying each of the crossing streets.
objects.artist: No machine-readable author provided. Macanese馬交人 assumed (based on copyright cla

objects.title: Handicraft
objects.description: Item production made completely by hand or with simple tools
objects.extract: A handicraft  is a traditional main sector of craft making and applies to a wide range of creative and design activities that are related to making things with one's hands and skill, including work with textiles, moldable and rigid materials, paper, plant fibers, clay, etc. One of the oldest handicraft is Dhokra; this is a sort of metal casting that has been

objects.title: Fur
objects.description: Soft, thick, hairy coat of a mammal
objects.extract: A fur is a soft, thick growth of hair that covers the skin of almost all mammals. It consists of a combination of oily guard hair on top and thick underfur beneath.
objects.artist: original by Lukas Kaffer (Super.lukas); crops and changes by JD

objects.title: Heller's broad-nosed bat
objects.extract: Heller's broad-nosed bat (Platyrrhinus helleri) is a bat species from South and Central America.

objects.title: Tableware
objects.description: Items used for setting a table and serving food
objects.extract: Tableware items are the dishware and utensils used for setting a table, serving food, and dining. The term includes cutlery, glassware, serving dishes, serving utensils, and other items used for practical as well as decorative purposes.

objects.title: Emergency light
objects.description: Backup light source used in power outages
objects.extract:
An emergency light is a battery-backed lighting device that switches on automatically when a building experiences a power outage. 
In the United States, emergency lights are standard in new commercial and high occupancy residential buildings, such as college dormitories, apartments, and hotels.

objects.title: Continuous track
objects.extract: Continuous track or tracked treads are a system of vehicle propulsion used in tracked vehicles, running on a continuous band of treads or track plates driven by two or more wheels. The large surface area of the tracks distributes the weight of the vehicle better than steel or rubber tyres on an equivalent vehicle, enabling continuous tracked vehicles to trav

objects.title: Gecko
objects.description: Lizards belonging to the suborder Gekkota
objects.extract: Geckos are small, mostly carnivorous lizards that have a wide distribution, found on every continent except Antarctica. Belonging to the suborder Gekkota, geckos are found in warm climates.

objects.title: Cabbage
objects.description: Leafy vegetable in the flowering plant family Brassicaceae
objects.extract: Cabbage, comprising several cultivars of Brassica oleracea, is a leafy green, red (purple), or white (pale green) biennial plant grown as an annual vegetable crop for its dense-leaved heads. It is descended from the wild cabbage (B. oleracea var.
objects.artist:
fir0002  flagstaffotos [at] gmail.com
		Canon 20D + Sigma 150mm f/2.8

objects.title: Truck
objects.extract: A truck (North American and Australian English) or lorry (British English) is a motor vehicle designed to transport freight, carry specialized payloads, or perform other utilitarian work. Trucks vary greatly in size, power, and configuration, but the vast majority feature body-on-frame construction, with a cabin that is independent of the payload portion of

objects.title: Phrygian cap
objects.description: Soft conical cap with the top pulled forward
objects.extract: The Phrygian cap ( ), also known as Thracian cap and liberty cap, is a soft conical cap with the apex bent over, associated in antiquity with several peoples in Eastern Europe, Anatolia, and Asia. The Phrygian cap was worn by Thracians, Dacians, Persians, Medes, Scythians, Trojans, and Phrygians after whom it is named.

objects.title: Hipposideros commersoni
objects.extract: Commerson's roundleaf bat (Macronycteris commersoni), also known as Commerson's leaf-nosed bat, is a species of bat endemic to Madagascar. It is named after French naturalist Philibert Commerson (1727–1773).

objects.title: Minivan
objects.description: Type of van designed for private use
objects.extract: A minivan, commonly known as a van, is a car classification for vehicles designed to transport passengers in the rear seating row(s), with reconfigurable seats in two or three rows. The equivalent classification in Europe is MPV (multi-purpose vehicle), people carrier, or M-segment.

objects.title: Poison dart frog
objects.extract: The poison dart frog (also known as the dart-poison frog, the poison frog or formerly known as the poison arrow frog) is the common name of a group of frogs in the family Dendrobatidae which are native to tropical Central and South America. These species are diurnal and often have brightly colored bodies.
objects.artist: V2, first published on his homepage youngester.com

objects.title: Areni-1 shoe
objects.extract: The Areni-1 shoe is a 5,500-year-old leather shoe that was found in 2008 in excellent condition in the Areni-1 cave located in the Vayots Dzor province of Armenia. It is a one-piece leather-hide shoe, the oldest piece of leather footwear in the world known to contemporary researchers.

objects.title: Dufuna canoe
objects.description: second oldest ship in the world

objects.title: Shoebill
objects.extract: The shoebill (Balaeniceps rex), also known as the whale-headed stork, whalebill, and shoe-billed stork, is a large long-legged wading bird. It is named for its enormous, shoe-shaped bill.

objects.title: Package handle
objects.extract: Package handles, or carriers, are used to help people use packaging. They are designed to simplify and to improve the ergonomics of lifting and carrying packages.

objects.title: Harp
objects.description: instrument in the collections of the Swedish Museum of Performing Arts (F475)

objects.title: Meerkat
objects.description: Species of mongoose from Southern Africa
objects.extract: The meerkat (Suricata suricatta) or suricate is a small mongoose found in southern Africa. It is characterised by a broad head, large eyes, a pointed snout, long legs, a thin tapering tail, and a brindled coat pattern.
objects.artist: Klaus Rassinger und Gerhard Cammerer, Museum Wiesbaden

objects.title: Steaming
objects.extract: Steaming is a method of cooking using steam. In steaming, the food is fully immersed in steam, with no air present, so that the steam condenses on the food, and then the condensate drips down away from the food.

objects.title: Dress
objects.description: dress by House of Worth (MET, C.I.64.5.1a–c)

objects.title: Boundary marker
objects.description: Physical marker that identifies a land boundary
objects.extract: A boundary marker, border marker, boundary stone, or border stone is a robust physical marker that identifies the start of a land boundary or the change in a boundary, especially a change in direction of a boundary. There are several other types of named border markers, known as boundary trees, pillars, monuments, obelisks, and corners.

objects.title: Komodo dragon
objects.extract: The Komodo dragon (Varanus komodoensis), also known as the Komodo monitor, is a large reptile of the monitor lizard family Varanidae that is endemic to the Indonesian islands of Komodo, Rinca, Flores, Gili Dasami, and Gili Motang. The largest extant population lives  within the Komodo National Park in Eastern Indonesia.

objects.title: Salty liquorice
objects.extract:
Salty liquorice, also known as salmiak liquorice, salmiac liquorice or (in Finland) salmiakki, is a variety of liquorice flavoured with salmiak salt (sal ammoniac; ammonium chloride), and is a common confection found in the Nordic countries, Benelux, and northern Germany. 
Salmiak salt gives salty liquorice an astringent, salty taste, akin to that of tannins

objects.title: Oriental Shorthair
objects.extract: The Oriental Shorthair is a breed of domestic cat that is developed from and closely related to the Siamese cat. It maintains the modern Siamese head and body type but appears in a wide range of coat colours and patterns.

objects.title: Pectenoniscus iuiuensis
objects.artist: Cardoso, Giovanna Monticelli et al. New cave species of Pectenoniscus Andersson,

objects.title: Road roller
objects.extract: A road roller (sometimes called a roller-compactor, or just roller) is a compactor-type engineering vehicle used to compact soil, gravel, concrete, or asphalt in the construction of roads and foundations. Similar rollers are used also at landfills or in agriculture.

objects.title: Nintendo
objects.extract: Nintendo Co., Ltd. is a Japanese multinational video game company headquartered in Kyoto.

objects.title: Milkshake
objects.extract: A milkshake (sometimes simply called a shake) is a sweet beverage made by blending milk, ice cream, and flavorings or sweeteners such as butterscotch, caramel sauce, chocolate syrup, or fruit syrup into a thick, sweet, cold mixture. It may also be made using a base made from non-dairy products, including plant milks such as almond milk, coconut milk, or soy
objects.artist: Lauren Michell Rabaino from San Luis Obispo, California, United States

objects.title: Tim Hortons
objects.description: Canadian multinational coffeehouse and restaurant chain
objects.extract: Tim Hortons Inc., known colloquially as Tim's, Timmies or Timmy's, is a Canadian multinational coffeehouse and restaurant chain with headquarters in Toronto; it serves coffee, donuts, sandwiches, breakfast egg muffins and other fast-food items. It is Canada's largest quick-service restaurant chain, with 6,043 restaurants in 14 countries.

objects.title: List of fur headgear
objects.extract: Below is a list of fur headgear (fur hats, fur caps, etc.). Some other kinds of hats can be lined or trimmed with fur, such as historical Scandinavian  karpus or kabuss/kabuds hats, or Spanish montera/montero caps.

objects.title: Segway
objects.extract: The Segway ( SEG-way) is a two-wheeled, self-balancing personal transporter invented by Dean Kamen. The name is a registered trademark of Segway Inc.

objects.title: Royal Canadian Mounted Police
objects.extract: The Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP; French: Gendarmerie royale du Canada, GRC) is the national police service of Canada. The RCMP is an agency of the Government of Canada; it also provides police services under contract to 11 provinces and territories (all but Ontario and Quebec), over 150 municipalities, and 600 Indigenous communities.

objects.title: Lizard
objects.extract: Lizard is the common name used for all squamate reptiles other than snakes (and to a lesser extent amphisbaenians or "worm lizards"), encompassing over 7,000 species, ranging across all continents except Antarctica, as well as most oceanic island chains. The grouping is paraphyletic as some lizards are more closely related to snakes than they are to other li

objects.title: Kendang
objects.extract: A kendang or gendang (Javanese: ꦏꦼꦤ꧀ꦝꦁ, romanized: kendhang, Sundanese: ᮊᮨᮔ᮪ᮓᮀ, romanized: kendang, Balinese: ᬓᬾᬦ᭄ᬤᬂ, romanized: kendang, Tausug/Bajau/Maranao: gandang, Bugis: gendrang and Makassar: gandrang or ganrang) is a two-headed drum used by people from the Indonesian Archipelago. The kendang is one of the primary instruments used in the gamelan ensem

objects.title: Puppet
objects.extract: A puppet is an object, often resembling a human, animal or mythical figure, that is animated or manipulated by a person called a puppeteer. Puppetry is an ancient form of theatre which dates back to the 5th century BC in ancient Greece.

objects.title: Syzygium aqueum (leaves)
objects.description: Leaves of a species of tree
objects.artist: This Photo was taken by Timothy A. Gonsalves.  Feel free to use my photos, but p

objects.title: SMS Brummer
objects.extract: SMS Brummer was the lead ship of the Brummer class of armored gunboats built for the German Kaiserliche Marine in the 1880s. The ship was ordered to serve in Germany's coastal defense system alongside the Sachsen-class ironclads and Wespe-class gunboats.

objects.title: Darevskia alpina
objects.extract:
Darevskia alpina is a species of lizard in the family Lacertidae.
It is found in the Greater Caucasus in Georgia and Russia.

objects.title: Snake
objects.extract: Snakes are elongated legless reptiles of the suborder Serpentes. Cladistically squamates, snakes are ectothermic, amniote vertebrates covered in overlapping scales much like other members of the group.

objects.title: Vacuum cleaner
objects.description: Device that sucks up dirt from a surface
objects.extract:
A vacuum cleaner, also known simply as a vacuum (or a hoover in the UK), is a device that uses suction, and often agitation, in order to remove dirt and other debris from carpets, hard floors, and other surfaces.
The dirt is collected into a dust bag or a plastic bin.

objects.title: Chinese cuisine
objects.extract: Chinese cuisine comprises cuisines originating from China, that varies with the country's large population and geographic diversity, as well as from Chinese people from other parts of the world. Due to the Chinese diaspora and the historical power of the country, Chinese cuisine has profoundly influenced other cuisines in Asia and beyond, with modifications

objects.title: Dumpling
objects.description: Food that consists of small pieces of dough
objects.extract: Dumplings are a broad class of dishes that consist of pieces of cooked dough (made from a variety of starchy sources), often wrapped around a filling. The dough can be based on bread, wheat or other flours, or potatoes, and it may be filled with meat, fish, tofu, cheese, vegetables, or a combination.
objects.artist: Dave Cook / Flickr user: Eating In Translation ( https://www.flickr.com/photos/e

objects.title: Liquid helium
objects.description: Liquid state of the element helium
objects.extract: Liquid helium is a physical state of helium at very low temperatures at standard atmospheric pressures. Liquid helium may show superfluidity.
objects.artist: I, AlfredLeitner, took this photograph as part of my movie "Liquid Helium,Superf

objects.title: Lesser Spotted Eagle
objects.extract: The lesser spotted eagle (Clanga pomarina) is a large Eastern European bird of prey. Like all typical eagles, it belongs to the family Accipitridae.
objects.artist: Ron Knight from Seaford, East Sussex, United Kingdom

objects.title: Annual growth cycle of grapevines
objects.description: Process that takes place in the vineyard each year
objects.extract: The annual growth cycle of grapevines is the process that takes place in the vineyard each year, beginning with bud break in the spring and culminating in leaf fall in autumn followed by winter dormancy. From a winemaking perspective, each step in the process plays a vital role in the development of grapes with ideal characteristics for making wine.
objects.artist:
fir0002  flagstaffotos [at] gmail.com
		Canon 20D + Tamron 28-75mm f/2.8

objects.title: Engraved gem
objects.extract: An engraved gem, frequently referred to as an intaglio (pl. intaglios, intagli), is a small and usually semi-precious gemstone that has been carved, in the Western tradition normally with images or inscriptions only on one face.

objects.title: Japanese tea ceremony
objects.extract: The Japanese tea ceremony is a cultural activity known as sadō/chadō (茶道, 'the way of tea') or chanoyu (茶の湯, 'hot water for tea'); the ceremony focuses on the presentation and serving of matcha (抹茶), powdered green tea, and this procedure is called temae (点前). Much less commonly, Japanese tea practice uses leaf tea, primarily sencha.
objects.artist: painted by 長谷川等伯, calligraphy by 春屋宗園

objects.title: Plague (disease)
objects.description: Disease caused by Yersinia pestis bacterium
objects.extract: Plague is an infectious disease caused by the bacterium Yersinia pestis. Symptoms include fever, weakness, headache and black lips.
objects.artist:
Photo Credit=
Content Providers= CDC/ Courtesy of Larry Stauffer, Oregon State P

objects.title: Glass gold-band mosaic bottle
objects.description: bottle, gold-band mosaic at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 30.115.16)

objects.title: Chess
objects.description: Traditional board game for two players
objects.extract: Chess is a board game for two players, played on a square board consisting of 64 squares arranged in an 8×8 grid. The players, referred to as "White" and "Black", each control sixteen pieces: one king, one queen, two rooks, two bishops, two knights, and eight pawns, with each piece type having a different pattern of movement.

objects.title: Screw extractor
objects.description: Tool for removing broken or seized screws
objects.extract: A screw extractor is a tool for removing broken or seized screws. There are two types: one has a spiral flute structure, commonly called an easy out after the trademarked name EZ-Out; the other has a straight flute structure.

objects.title: Miso soup
objects.extract: Miso soup (味噌汁 or お味噌汁, miso-shiru or omiso-shiru; お-/o- being honorific) is a traditional Japanese soup consisting of miso in a dashi stock. It is commonly served as part of an ichijū-sansai (一汁三菜) meal 'one soup, three dishes' consisting of rice, soup, and side dishes.

objects.title: Gaiwan
objects.description: Bowl for the infusion of tea leaves
objects.extract: A gaiwan (simplified Chinese: 盖碗; traditional Chinese: 蓋碗; ) or zhong (盅) is a Chinese lidded bowl without a handle, used for the infusion of tea leaves and the consumption of tea. It was invented during the Ming dynasty.

objects.title: Spindle-back armchair
objects.description: furniture highlighted in The MET collection

objects.title: Malting
objects.description: Process of steeping, germinating, and drying grain to convert it into malt
objects.extract: Malting is the process of steeping, germinating, and drying grain to convert it into malt. Germination and sprouting involve a number of enzymes to produce the changes from seed to seedling and the malt producer stops this stage of the process when the required enzymes are optimal.

objects.title: Paul Poiret
objects.extract: Alexandre Paul Poiret (French pronunciation: [alɛksɑ̃dʁ pɔl pwaʁe]; 20 April 1879 – 30 April 1944) was a French fashion designer and master couturier during the first two decades of the 20th century. He was the founder of his namesake haute couture house.

objects.title: Police box
objects.extract: A police box is a public telephone kiosk or callbox for the use of members of the police, or for members of the public to contact the police. It was used in some countries, most widely in the United Kingdom throughout the 20th century from the early 1920s.

objects.title: Minsk Tractor Works
objects.extract: Minsk Tractor Works (Belarusian: Мінскі трактарны завод, romanized: Minski traktarny zavod; Russian: Минский тракторный завод, romanized: Minskiy traktorniy zavod; MTZ) is a Belarusian agricultural machinery manufacturer with headquarters in Minsk, Belarus. Minsk Tractor Works is one of the main tractor factories in the country.

objects.title: Watch strap
objects.description: Bracelet that straps a watch to the wrist
objects.extract: A watch strap, watch band, watch bracelet or watch belt is a bracelet that straps a wrist watch onto the wrist. Watch straps may be made of leather, plastic, polyurethane, silicone, rubber, FKM,  cloth, or metal, sometimes in combination.

objects.title: Weevil
objects.extract: Weevils are beetles belonging to the superfamily Curculionoidea, known for their very long snouts. They are usually small – less than 6 mm (1⁄4 in) in length – and herbivorous.

objects.title: Calculator
objects.extract:
A calculator is typically a portable electronic device used to perform calculations, ranging from basic arithmetic to complex mathematics.
The first solid-state electronic calculator was created in the early 1960s.
objects.artist: Yordan (talk) (Transferred by piotr433/Originally uploaded by Yordan)

objects.title: Jewellery
objects.extract: Jewellery (or jewelry in American English) consists of decorative items worn for personal adornment such as brooches, rings, necklaces, earrings, pendants, bracelets, and cufflinks. Jewellery may be attached to the body or the clothes.

objects.title: Oscar Niemeyer
objects.extract: Oscar Ribeiro de Almeida Niemeyer Soares Filho (15 December 1907 – 5 December 2012), known as Oscar Niemeyer (Brazilian Portuguese: [ˈoskaʁ ni.eˈmajeʁ]), was a Brazilian architect considered to be one of the key figures in the development of modern architecture. Niemeyer was best known for his design of civic buildings for Brasília, a planned city that becam

objects.title: Nail gun
objects.extract: A nail gun, nailgun or nailer is a form of hammer used to drive nails into wood or other materials. It is usually driven by compressed air (pneumatic), electromagnetism, highly flammable gases such as butane or propane, or, for powder-actuated tools, a small explosive charge.

objects.title: Pocketknife
objects.description: Folding knife that can be carried in a pocket
objects.extract: A pocketknife (also spelled pocket knife) is a knife with one or more blades that fold into the handle. It is also known as a jackknife, or a folding knife.

objects.title: Royal Canadian Mounted Police
objects.extract: The Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP; French: Gendarmerie royale du Canada, GRC) is the national police service of Canada. The RCMP is an agency of the Government of Canada; it also provides police services under contract to 11 provinces and territories (all but Ontario and Quebec), over 150 municipalities, and 600 Indigenous communities.

objects.title: Earth auger
objects.description: Device for digging holes on the ground
objects.extract: An earth auger, earth drill, or post-hole auger is a drilling tool used for making holes in the ground. It typically consists of a rotating vertical metal rod or pipe with one or more blades attached at the lower end, that cut or scrape the soil.

objects.title: Khepresh
objects.extract: The khepresh (ḫprš) was an ancient Egyptian royal headdress. It is also known as the blue crown or war crown.

objects.title: Porcelain
objects.extract: Porcelain (), also called china, is a ceramic material made by heating raw materials, generally including kaolinite, in a kiln to temperatures between 1,200 and 1,400 °C (2,200 and 2,600 °F). The greater strength and translucence of porcelain, relative to other types of pottery, arise mainly from vitrification and the formation of the mineral mullite within

objects.title: Blu-ray
objects.extract: Blu-ray (Blu-ray Disc or BD) is a digital optical disc data storage format designed to supersede the DVD format. It was invented and developed in the early-mid 2000s and released worldwide on June 20, 2006, capable of storing several hours of high-definition video (HDTV 720p and 1080p).

objects.title: Hippopotamus
objects.extract: The hippopotamus (Hippopotamus amphibius; ; pl.: hippopotamuses), often shortened to hippo (pl.: hippos), further qualified as the common hippopotamus, Nile hippopotamus and river hippopotamus, is a large semiaquatic mammal native to sub-Saharan Africa. It is one of only two extant species in the family Hippopotamidae, the other being the pygmy hippopotamus

objects.title: Shark
objects.extract: Sharks are a group of elasmobranch cartilaginous fishes characterized by a ribless endoskeleton, dermal denticles, five to seven gill slits on each side, and pectoral fins that are not fused to the head. Modern sharks are classified within the division Selachii and are the sister group to the Batomorphi (rays and skates).

objects.title: Chocolate milk
objects.extract: Chocolate milk is a type of flavoured milk made by mixing cocoa powder with milk (either dairy or plant-based). It is a food pairing in which the milk's mouthfeel masks the dietary fibres of the (non-fat) cocoa solids of cocoa powder.

objects.title: Turkish crescent
objects.extract: A Turkish crescent, also called Turkish jingle or a Jingling Johnny (a smaller version is called a Çevgen; Turkish: Çağana; German: Schellenbaum;  French: Chapeau chinois or Pavillon chinois),  is a percussion instrument traditionally used by military bands internationally. In some contexts it also serves as a battle trophy or object of veneration.

objects.title: Hammer
objects.extract: A  hammer is a tool, most often a hand tool, consisting of a weighted "head" fixed to a long handle that is swung to deliver an impact to a small area of an object. This can be, for example, to drive nails into wood, to shape metal (as with a forge), or to crush rock.

objects.title: Performance poetry
objects.extract: Performance poetry is poetry that is specifically composed for or during a performance before an audience. It covers a variety of styles and genres.

objects.title: Seahorse
objects.extract: A seahorse (also written sea-horse and sea horse) is any of 46 species of small marine bony fish in the genus Hippocampus. The genus name comes from the Ancient Greek hippókampos (ἱππόκαμπος), itself from híppos (ἵππος) meaning "horse" and kámpos (κάμπος) meaning "sea monster" or "sea animal".

objects.title: Inclined plane
objects.extract: An inclined plane, also known as a ramp, is a flat supporting surface tilted at an angle from the vertical direction, with one end higher than the other, used as an aid for raising or lowering a load. The inclined plane is one of the six classical simple machines defined by Renaissance scientists.

objects.title: Hippopotamus
objects.extract: The hippopotamus (Hippopotamus amphibius; ; pl.: hippopotamuses), often shortened to hippo (pl.: hippos), further qualified as the common hippopotamus, Nile hippopotamus and river hippopotamus, is a large semiaquatic mammal native to sub-Saharan Africa. It is one of only two extant species in the family Hippopotamidae, the other being the pygmy hippopotamus

objects.title: Adhesive
objects.description: Non-metallic material used to bond various materials together
objects.extract:
Adhesive, also known as glue, cement, mucilage, or paste, is any non-metallic substance applied to one or both surfaces of two separate items that binds them together and resists their separation.
The use of adhesives offers certain advantages over other binding techniques such as sewing, mechanical fastenings, and welding.

objects.title: Bombus sandersoni
objects.extract: Bombus sandersoni is a species of bumblebee known commonly as the Sanderson bumblebee. It is native to North America, where it occurs across Canada and in the eastern United States.

objects.title: Hem
objects.extract: In sewing, a hem is a garment finishing method in which the edge of a piece of cloth is folded and sewn to prevent unravelling of the fabric and adjust the length of the piece in garments, such as at the end of the sleeve or the bottom of the garment.

objects.title: Wi-Fi
objects.extract: Wi-Fi () is a family of wireless network protocols based on the IEEE 802.11 family of standards, which are commonly used for local area networking of devices and Internet access, allowing nearby digital devices to exchange data by radio waves. These are the most widely used computer networks, used globally in home and small office networks to link devices an

objects.title: Toyota Highlander
objects.extract:
The Toyota Highlander (Japanese: トヨタ・ハイランダー, Hepburn: Toyota Hairandā), also known as the Toyota Kluger (Japanese: トヨタ・クルーガー, Hepburn: Toyota Kurūgā) in some markets, is a mid-size crossover SUV with three-row seating produced by Toyota since 2000.
The Highlander was announced in April 2000 at the New York International Auto Show, arriving in Japan in late 2

objects.title: Construction
objects.description: Process of building or assembling a building or infrastructure
objects.extract: Construction is the process involved in delivering buildings, infrastructure, industrial facilities, and associated activities through to the end of their life. It typically starts with planning, financing, and design that continues until the asset is built and ready for use.

objects.title: Dala horse
objects.description: Traditional Swedish carved, painted wooden horse statuette
objects.extract: A Dala horse (Swedish: dalahäst) or Dalecarlian horse is a traditional carved, painted wooden statuette of a horse originating in the Swedish province of Dalarna (Dalecarlia). The Dala horse has been widely used as a toy for children.

objects.title: Door knocker
objects.description: Device fixed to a door to make a noise
objects.extract: A door knocker is an item of door furniture that allows people outside a house or other dwelling or building to alert those inside to their presence.  A door knocker has a part fixed to the door, and a part (usually metal) which is attached to the door by a hinge, and may be lifted and used to strike a plate fitted to the door, or the door itself, making a n

objects.title: Bee
objects.extract: Bees are winged insects that form a monophyletic clade Anthophila within the superfamily Apoidea of the order Hymenoptera, with over 20,000 known species in seven recognized families. Some species – including honey bees, bumblebees, and stingless bees – are social insects living in highly hierarchical colonies, while over 90% of bee species – including mason

objects.title: Vitis vinifera
objects.description: Species of flowering plant in the grape vine family
objects.extract: Vitis vinifera, the common grape vine, is a species of flowering plant, native to the Mediterranean region, Central Europe, and southwestern Asia, from Morocco and Portugal north to southern Germany and east to northern Iran. As of 2012, there were between 5,000 and 10,000 varieties of Vitis vinifera grapes though only a few are of commercial significance fo

objects.title: Dango
objects.extract: Dango (団子) is a Japanese dumpling made with regular rice flour and glutinous rice flour. They are usually made in round shapes, and three to five pieces are served on a skewer, which is called kushi-dango (串団子).

objects.title: Adrian helmet
objects.extract: The Adrian helmet (French: Casque Adrian) is a combat helmet designed by Louis Adrian for the French Army during World War I. Its original version, the M15, was the first standard helmet of the French Army and was designed when millions of French troops were engaged in trench warfare, and head wounds from falling shrapnel generated by indirect fire became a

objects.title: Anemometer
objects.extract: In meteorology, an anemometer (from Ancient Greek  άνεμος (ánemos) 'wind' and  μέτρον (métron) 'measure') is a device that measures wind speed and direction. It is a common instrument used in weather stations.

objects.title: Dung beetle
